name: Tajuña Greenway and Nuevo Baztán Loop
name_original: Tielmes, Ambite, Pozuelo del Rey – ruta circular
description: This moderate 33.2 miles (53.5 km) road cycling route connects Tielmes, Ambite, and Pozuelo del Rey, offering scenic views and historic town
region_intro: Road cycling around Tielmes, located in the Community of Madrid, offers diverse terrain for cyclists. The region is characterized by a mix of fertile river plains along the Tajuña River and more arid uplands, providing varied cycling experiences. Routes often feature gentle climbs, steep descents, and flat sections, traversing landscapes of olive groves, vineyards, and cereal fields. This area is integrated into the CiclaMadrid network, highlighting its appeal for road cyclists. Best road cycling routes around Tielmes The most popular road cycling route is Tajuña Greenway and Campo Real Loop, a 45.9 miles (73.8 km) trail that takes 3 hours 37 minutes to complete. This route connects Tielmes with nearby towns and utilizes sections of the Tajuña Greenway. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Tajuña Greenway and Nuevo Baztán Loop, a moderate 33.2 miles (53.5 km) path. This route incorporates the Tajuña Greenway and passes through varied landscapes including the town of Nuevo Baztán. Local road cyclists also love the Tajuña Greenway – Tajuña Greenway loop from Tielmes, a 52.9 miles (85.1 km) trail leading through the Tajuña River valley, often completed in about 3 hours 52 minutes. Road cycling around Tielmes is defined by the Tajuña River, the Vía Verde del Tajuña, and extensive olive groves. The network offers options for moderate and difficult ability levels, catering to various cycling preferences. The routes in Tielmes are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.75 stars from 5 reviews. More than 1000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Tielmes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Tielmes?
A: Tielmes offers a selection of 10 road cycling routes, catering to various preferences. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.75 stars from 5 reviews.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes near Tielmes?
A: The region around Tielmes features diverse terrain, from fertile river plains along the Tajuña River to more arid uplands. Cyclists can expect a mix of gentle climbs, steep descents, and flatter sections, often traversing landscapes of olive groves, vineyards, and cereal fields. The area is integrated into the CiclaMadrid network, ensuring varied and engaging rides.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking a challenge, Tielmes offers 2 difficult-grade road cycling routes. One notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20135569" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monument to the Eyes – Tajuña Greenway loop from Tielmes&lt;/a&gt;, which covers approximately 134.5 km with over 1100 meters of elevation gain.

Q: Are there easier road cycling options for beginners or less experienced riders?
A: While most routes are moderate, the Vía Verde del Tajuña (Greenway of the Tajuña) offers mostly flat and scenic sections, making it suitable for less experienced riders or those looking for a more relaxed pace. Routes that incorporate this greenway, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977086965" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tajuña Greenway and Nuevo Baztán Loop&lt;/a&gt;, provide a good balance of accessibility and scenic beauty.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Road cycling routes around Tielmes often feature the scenic Vía Verde del Tajuña, following the Tajuña River. You might also encounter viewpoints like Mirador del Valle and Mirador de la Ermita, offering panoramic vistas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4899737" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tajuña Greenway Section&lt;/a&gt; itself is a significant natural feature. The area is also characterized by extensive olive groves and riverine ecosystems.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest accessible by road bike?
A: Yes, several routes pass near points of interest. For instance, you might cycle past the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5434256" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Civil War Caves&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3219959" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chapel of the Holy Children&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes also lead through towns where you can find local delicacies like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2676541" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chocolate Palmeras from Morata de Tajuña&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Tielmes?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Tielmes are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977182618" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tajuña Greenway and Campo Real Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31877587" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tajuña Greenway – Tajuña Greenway loop from Tielmes&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go road cycling in Tielmes?
A: The region's climate generally makes spring and autumn ideal for road cycling, offering pleasant temperatures and less extreme weather. Summers can be hot, especially midday, while winters are generally mild but can have cooler days. Checking local weather forecasts before your ride is always recommended.

Q: What do other cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Tielmes?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.75 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain, the scenic beauty of the Tajuña River valley, and the well-maintained roads that are part of the CiclaMadrid network. The mix of gentle climbs and flat sections, along with charming towns, also receives positive feedback.

Q: Are there any routes that combine road cycling with sections of the Tajuña Greenway?
A: Absolutely. Several routes strategically incorporate sections of the Vía Verde del Tajuña, a former railway line converted into a greenway. This allows for a varied experience, blending smooth road cycling with the tranquil, mostly flat paths of the greenway. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977182618" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tajuña Greenway and Campo Real Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example of such a route.

Q: Can I find places to stop for refreshments or food along the routes?
A: Many road cycling routes around Tielmes pass through or near small towns and villages, such as Morata de Tajuña, Campo Real, and Nuevo Baztán. These towns typically offer cafes, restaurants, and shops where you can refuel and take a break. It's advisable to plan your stops in advance, especially on longer rides.

Q: What is the typical elevation gain for road cycling routes in Tielmes?
A: The elevation gain varies significantly depending on the route. Moderate routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977086965" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tajuña Greenway and Nuevo Baztán Loop&lt;/a&gt; might have around 420 meters of ascent, while more challenging routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20135569" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monument to the Eyes – Tajuña Greenway loop from Tielmes&lt;/a&gt;, can feature over 1100 meters of climbing, offering a good workout for those seeking significant elevation.
tips: Nice bridge on the greenway of the Tajuña. It was originally built to cross over the old railway track which has now been converted into a fantastic cycle path.
It is a quiet and pretty section. The pine trees give a lot of shade, perfect when it is very hot in summer.

Villar del Olmo Town Hall has a QR code system, and you can discover the 21 points of interest, both scenic and historical, through your mobile or tablet, with information and videos from a drone view. There are many routes and many places of interest.

Nuevo Baztán is 40 minutes from Madrid and has entered the list of "The Most Beautiful Towns in Spain". The Goyeneche palace is in a very beautiful area in front of a square full of trees. Goyeneche's palace and residence forms a façade with the Church, forming the central nucleus of this monument. There is a tourist office where they give you a map and QR codes with a tour to do around the town and all the information about those points.
The Hermitage of the Virgen de Oliva de Valdilecha was built in the 17th century. It is located in a place that offers a magnificent view of the valley.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Tielmes, Ambite, Pozuelo del Rey circular route?
faqs.answer: As this is a circular route connecting three towns, you have flexibility. You can typically find parking in the town centers of Tielmes, Ambite, or Pozuelo del Rey to begin your ride. Look for public parking areas within these villages.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for this racebike route?
faqs.answer: The route is primarily designed for racebikes, meaning it largely follows paved roads. You can expect a mix of road types with rolling hills. Sections that align with the Stone Arch Bridge on the Tajuña Greenway are known for their asphalt conditions, though be aware of potential speed bumps. While the general area can have varied surfaces, this specific route focuses on road cycling.

faqs.question: Is the Tielmes, Ambite, Pozuelo del Rey circular route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate and covers approximately 53.5 km with about 420 meters of elevation gain. It requires a good level of fitness for racebiking. While not extremely challenging, beginners might find the distance and rolling hills demanding. Consider your current fitness level before attempting the full loop.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter a mix of picturesque riverine and rural landscapes, along with historic towns. Highlights include the Stone Arch Bridge on the Tajuña Greenway, the Pine Forest Trail, and the charming Villar del Olmo Town Center and Points of Interest. You'll also pass near the historic Goyeneche Palace of Nuevo Baztán and the Ermita de la Virgen de la Oliva de Valdilecha.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Madrid region generally offers excellent cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring (April-May) and Autumn (September-October) are often ideal, with pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can be very hot, so if cycling then, it's best to start early in the morning to avoid the midday heat.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other well-known cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route align with parts of the Vía Verde del Tajuña, which makes up a significant portion of the trail. You'll also briefly intersect with the Senda de Valmores and the Camino Santiaguista de Uclés.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route in Comarca de Las Vegas?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route. The towns and greenways are publicly accessible. However, always respect local regulations, especially when passing through private land or protected areas, and adhere to traffic laws on public roads.

faqs.question: Is the Tielmes, Ambite, Pozuelo del Rey circular route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route primarily follows public roads and greenways, which are generally accessible, it's a racebike route. Cycling with dogs, especially on a racebike, can be challenging and potentially unsafe for both you and your pet. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure it is well-trained, can keep pace, and is always on a leash or in a suitable carrier, especially in populated areas or near traffic.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or get refreshments along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route passes through Tielmes, Ambite, and Pozuelo del Rey, you'll find opportunities for cafes, restaurants, and shops in these towns to refuel and grab refreshments.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this racebike tour?
faqs.answer: For this moderate racebike tour, ensure you have your racebike in good condition, a helmet, appropriate cycling attire, water bottles (enough for ~2.5 hours of riding), snacks, a basic repair kit (spare tube, pump, tire levers), and a charged phone. Sunscreen and sunglasses are also recommended, especially during warmer months.

faqs.question: Are there any challenging sections or specific considerations for this route?
faqs.answer: The route is rated moderate, with rolling hills. While mostly paved, some sections of greenways might have speed bumps, requiring attention. Be mindful of traffic when on public roads, especially when entering or exiting towns. Always prioritize safety and be aware of your surroundings.
